title: bromus lanceolatus var. danthoniae
intro: Bromus lanceolatus var. danthoniae, commonly known as Danthonia fescue or blue oat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to North America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Bromus lanceolatus var. danthoniae include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies to create salves, creams, and oils for topical applications.
